userStories.title: Add tdd-simple test strategy type and routing
userStories.description:
Introduce `tdd-simple` as a new TestStrategy. This is a single-session TDD approach where one agent writes failing tests first, then implements to make them pass — no session isolation.

Changes:
1. `src/config/types.ts`: Add `"tdd-simple"` to `TestStrategy` union type
2. `src/routing/strategies/keyword.ts`: Change `simple` complexity default from `test-after` to `tdd-simple` (line ~121). `test-after` remains available for manual routing (refactors, deletions, config-only changes).
3. `src/routing/strategies/llm-prompts.ts`: Update the LLM routing prompt to include `tdd-simple` as an option with guidance on when to use it vs test-after
4. Update any Zod schemas or config validation that enumerate TestStrategy values

The strategy spectrum becomes:
- `test-after`: Non-TDD work (refactors, deletions, config changes, documentation)
- `tdd-simple`: Simple stories — single agent, TDD discipline, 1 session
- `three-session-tdd-lite`: Medium stories — 3 sessions, lite isolation
- `three-session-tdd`: Complex stories — 3 sessions, strict isolation

Run: NAX_SKIP_PRECHECK=1 bun test test/unit/routing/ test/unit/config/ --timeout=60000
Commit: 'feat: add tdd-simple test strategy type and routing'
acceptanceCriteria: TestStrategy type includes 'tdd-simple'
Keyword router maps simple complexity to tdd-simple (not test-after)
LLM routing prompt describes tdd-simple and when to use it vs test-after
All config validation / Zod schemas accept tdd-simple
Existing routing tests updated for new default
TypeScript and lint checks pass

routing.reasoning: Type system extension + routing config updates with no novel logic; straightforward additions to existing enums and prompts

userStories.title: Add tdd-simple prompt section and PromptBuilder support
userStories.description:
Create the prompt content for the `tdd-simple` role. This is a single-session prompt that instructs the agent to follow TDD discipline: write failing tests first, confirm they fail, then implement to make them pass.

Changes:
1. `src/prompts/sections/role-task.ts`: Add `tdd-simple` case to `buildRoleTaskSection()`. The prompt should:
   - Instruct: 'Write failing tests FIRST for the acceptance criteria'
   - Instruct: 'Run the tests to confirm they fail (red phase)'
   - Instruct: 'Implement the minimum code to make tests pass (green phase)'
   - Instruct: 'Refactor if needed while keeping tests green'
   - Include the git commit instruction (same as implementer)
   - NOT include isolation rules (agent can touch both src/ and test/)
2. `src/prompts/sections/isolation.ts`: Add `tdd-simple` case that returns NO isolation rules (empty string or a note saying 'You may modify both src/ and test/ files')
3. `src/prompts/types.ts`: Add `tdd-simple` to `PromptRole` type
4. `src/prompts/builder.ts`: Ensure `tdd-simple` works through the existing build() flow
5. Update `src/cli/prompts.ts` to include `tdd-simple` in the `nax prompts -f <feature>` output

Run: NAX_SKIP_PRECHECK=1 bun test test/unit/prompts/ --timeout=60000
Commit: 'feat: add tdd-simple prompt section'
acceptanceCriteria: PromptRole type includes 'tdd-simple'
buildRoleTaskSection('tdd-simple') returns TDD-disciplined prompt with red-green-refactor instructions
tdd-simple prompt includes git commit instruction
buildIsolationSection('tdd-simple') returns no isolation (agent can modify src/ and test/)
PromptBuilder.for('tdd-simple') produces valid prompt with all sections
nax prompts -f outputs tdd-simple prompt when story is routed to it
All existing prompt tests still pass
TypeScript and lint checks pass

userStories.title: Wire tdd-simple execution path in session runner and pipeline
userStories.description:
Connect the tdd-simple strategy to the execution pipeline so stories routed to it actually run as a single TDD session.

Changes:
1. `src/pipeline/stages/execution.ts`: When `testStrategy === 'tdd-simple'`, run a single session (not 3-session TDD). Use the `tdd-simple` PromptBuilder role.
2. `src/tdd/session-runner.ts` or create `src/execution/tdd-simple-runner.ts`: Single session that builds a tdd-simple prompt (with story context and conventions) and runs one agent session. Returns a result compatible with the pipeline.
3. `src/pipeline/stages/prompt.ts`: Handle `tdd-simple` strategy — build prompt using `PromptBuilder.for('tdd-simple')`
4. Ensure pipeline stages (verify, review, etc.) work correctly after a tdd-simple session — they should behave the same as after any other strategy.
5. Update `src/execution/lifecycle/headless-formatter.ts` if needed to display tdd-simple sessions correctly.

Key: tdd-simple is essentially like single-session but with a TDD-specific prompt. It should reuse the single-session execution path where possible.

Run: NAX_SKIP_PRECHECK=1 bun test test/unit/ --timeout=60000 --bail
Commit: 'feat: wire tdd-simple execution path'
acceptanceCriteria: Stories with testStrategy tdd-simple execute as a single agent session
tdd-simple session uses PromptBuilder.for('tdd-simple') with story context and conventions
Pipeline verify/review stages run normally after tdd-simple session
Headless formatter displays tdd-simple sessions correctly
No regression in existing test-after or three-session-tdd execution paths
Full unit test suite passes with 0 failures
TypeScript and lint checks pass
